name: TinyUser Vietnamese Persona Specification v2
approach: 3 anchor fields sampled from real census data, then Qwen generates the remaining fields as a coherent persona. No abstract behavioral characteristics — every field is human-readable and directly reportable.

anchor_distributions.description: These 3 fields are sampled from real GSO/census data. They are the ONLY fields generated statistically. Everything else is LLM-generated conditioned on these anchors.

age_bracket.source: UN World Population Prospects 2024, GSO 2024 census. Restricted to adults 18+.

city.source: GSO 2024 intercensal census, Wikipedia provinces of Vietnam (2024 population data). Top 20 cities/provinces by population, plus a 'rural_other' bucket for remaining ~40% of population.
city.note: For 'rural_other', Qwen should generate a plausible smaller province/town name from remaining provinces.

llm_generated_fields.description: Given the 3 anchors (age, gender, city), Qwen generates ALL of the following as one coherent persona. These fields are directly reportable — a marketing team can read and act on every one.

job_title.note: Must be realistic for the age + city combination. A 22-year-old in rural Ha Tinh is not a 'VP of Engineering'.

industry.note: Must match job title and city. Da Nang skews tourism, Binh Duong skews manufacturing, HCMC is diverse.

company_size.note: Farmers, street vendors, retirees = 'not_applicable'. Factory workers = 'large' or 'enterprise'.

education_level.note: GSO 2024: ~28% of labor force holds training certificates. University education concentrated in urban areas.

income_bracket.note: GSO 2024: average monthly salary ~7.7M VND. Agriculture workers earn lowest. Must correlate with job + city.

primary_platform.note: DataReportal 2025: Facebook 95.4% of internet users, Zalo 86%, YouTube 80%, TikTok 53%. Older rural = Zalo/Facebook. Young urban = TikTok/Google.

search_language.note: Vast majority search in Vietnamese. English mainly for tech workers, educated urban youth, international-facing roles.

examples: Can't find a reliable supplier for my shop
Too much paperwork at the bank branch
My kids' school fees keep going up
Commute from Binh Duong to HCMC takes 2 hours
I don't understand the new tax regulations
top_3_frustrations.note: These are the CORE of the simulation. They must be specific, realistic, and tied to this person's actual life — not generic. A farmer's frustrations are completely different from a software engineer's.

generation_prompt_template.description: This is the prompt sent to Qwen for each persona. The 3 anchors are filled in, Qwen generates the rest as JSON.
generation_prompt_template.system: You are generating realistic Vietnamese personas for market research. Each persona must be a coherent, believable person. All fields must be consistent with each other and with the anchor demographics provided. Output valid JSON only, no explanation.
generation_prompt_template.note: Batch 5-10 personas per call for efficiency. Qwen should output a JSON array.

batching_strategy.description: To generate 100K personas efficiently on Modal:

output_schema.description: Final persona as stored in personas.json. Every field is directly usable in the report.

top_3_frustrations: Guest complaints pile up and there's no system to track them
I manually update room availability across 3 booking platforms
Staff scheduling is done on paper and always has conflicts

report_output.description: When segments are formed from matching personas, the report labels use THESE fields — not abstract characteristics. Every segment name is actionable.

example_segments.name: Hotel managers in Da Nang (25-44, university-educated)
example_segments.top_query: phần mềm quản lý khách sạn

example_segments.name: Bank tellers in HCMC (25-34, frustrated with paperwork)
example_segments.top_query: công cụ tự động hóa tài liệu ngân hàng

pre_computed_once: 1. Sample 100K (age, gender, city) triplets from anchor distributions
2. Qwen generates full personas with frustrations (Modal, ~$5-8, ~15 min)
per_product_run: 1. Claude generates product problem space (~20-30 problems) — 1 API call
3. Cosine similarity: 100K persona embeddings × 30 product embeddings — <1 second
4. Filter qualifying personas by threshold — instant
5. Qwen generates search queries for qualifying personas only — Modal, ~$1-3
6. TinyFish runs real Google searches — ~$13.50, ~2 minutes
7. Generate report with human-readable segment labels — instant
